deep_dive.title: Abstracting Cross-Domain Action Sequences into Interpretable Workflows
deep_dive.abstract: Sequential or time-stamped interaction logs provide objective records of digital application usage, yet their granularity and noise often obscure meaningful insights into people's work. Such insights are essential for improving digital products in ways grounded in real-world user interactions. Prior research has applied deep learning models to cluster user actions into high-level activities, but these approaches are highly sensitive to noise and struggle to generalize across applications. To address this limitation, we introduce WorkflowView, a framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to abstract low-level action sequences into high-level activities. We establish the effectiveness and generality of our approach across three distinct, challenging sequential tasks and diverse domains: (a) zero-shot task description reconstruction from browser logs (achieving high semantic similarity, $μ_{sim} = 0.91$), (b) few-shot student dropout prediction using MOOC interaction logs (reaching weighted $F_1 = 0.90$ with only five few-shot examples), and (c) anonymized, privacy-preserving analysis of AI tool integration within document workflows in Microsoft Word. Our work demonstrates that LLM-based abstraction is a robust and efficient path forward for transforming low-level behavioral data into high-level, interpretable, and actionable insights. We also discuss practical considerations for deploying LLM-based inferences within logging infrastructures, including computational efficiency and user privacy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLMs for clustering action sequences into high-level activities
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型将动作序列聚类为高层活动
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型将动作序列聚类为高层活动
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型将动作序列聚类为高层活动
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces WorkflowView, an LLM-based framework to abstract low-level action sequences into high-level activities, demonstrating effective clustering across domains.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对用户交互日志中的噪声和粒度问题，提出WorkflowView框架，利用大语言模型将低层动作序列抽象为高层活动。该方法在三个不同领域的数据集上验证了有效性和泛化能力，为使用LLM进行跨领域聚类提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对用户交互日志中的噪声和粒度问题，提出WorkflowView框架，利用大语言模型将低层动作序列抽象为高层活动。该方法在三个不同领域的数据集上验证了有效性和泛化能力，为使用LLM进行跨领域聚类提供了新思路。
